title: Writing
description: Technical articles, field notes, and curriculum material across smart contracts, Polkadot infrastructure, and frontend engineering.

entries.title: Mastering ink! Smart Contracts by Building a Wishlist Contract
entries.summary: A guided walkthrough of ink! v6 fundamentals using a realistic wishlist contract, from storage design and message patterns to testing and deployment.

entries.title: The Current Status of Decentralized Data Storage
entries.summary: A practical comparison of centralized storage, IPFS, and Filecoin, with a focus on availability, content addressing, and storage market incentives.

entries.title: 5 Tips to Writing Cleaner and Manageable React Applications
entries.summary: Lessons from real React codebases on structure, hooks, classes, state management, and styling choices that keep collaboration sustainable.

entries.title: Blockchain Account Models: Accounting vs UTXO Models
entries.summary: A conceptual comparison between account-based and UTXO-based chains, including state transition tradeoffs, privacy, and scalability.

entries.title: Understanding SCALE Encoding for Substrate Networks
entries.summary: An explanation of SCALE from first principles, including type-driven encoding, compact values, metadata, and ink! contract interactions.

entries.title: Frontend PAPI Interaction with Solidity and EVM Chains
entries.summary: A field guide to calling Solidity contracts on Polkadot smart contract environments from a frontend using PAPI, wallet integrations, and ABI encoding.

entries.title: Making Sense of Accounts in ink! v6
entries.summary: A practical explanation of the H160 account model in ink! v6 and how to convert SS58 identities into the EVM-compatible addresses contracts now expect.

entries.title: Implementing XCM in Polkadot Hub Using the XCM Precompile
entries.summary: A practical guide to using the XCM precompile from Solidity on Asset Hub, including message generation, precompile calls, and runtime execution flow.

entries.title: Web3Bridge Rust/Ink! Development Curriculum
entries.summary: A structured training map for Rust and ink! developers, covering progressively deeper contract patterns from foundations to cross-contract and cross-consensus flows.
